summary: Tesla's Autopilot AI is the most token-efficient AI in the world, running superhuman tasks at near-human power consumption
summary_extended: Baker corroborates Antonio Gracias's claim that Tesla is today the most advanced real-world AI deployment in the world, arguing that Tesla's Autopilot system — running on its custom A4 chip with no cloud connectivity — performs the most cognitively complex task humans undertake (driving) at approximately 20–50 watts of power consumption, comparable to the human brain's energy usage. This makes Tesla's edge AI the most token-efficient real-world AI system in existence, outperforming cloud-dependent large language models on a power-per-task basis. Baker and Gracias argue this capability advantage is not widely appreciated by the market, which tends to frame Tesla as an EV company rather than an AI company. The investment implication is that Tesla's edge AI lead creates a structural platform advantage that extends naturally into humanoid robotics via Optimus, which would leverage the same perception and inference stack already proven at scale in vehicles. The thesis is contrarian in that it positions TSLA as the world's leading applied AI company rather than a car manufacturer, suggesting the market is significantly mispricing the AI optionality embedded in the stock.
detail: Antonio Gracias (corroborated by Baker) argues Tesla Autopilot running on an A4 chip with no cloud access performs the most complex task humans do (driving) at ~20-50W — comparable to the human brain. This makes Tesla the most advanced edge AI deployment and positions Optimus as a natural extension of this capability advantage.
confidence_evidence: Stated as a strong claim by Gracias with Baker's agreement, but relies on internal analysis; specific wattage figures are approximate
quote_fragment: Tesla actually is today the most advanced AI in the world

ticker_audit.reasoning: The entire thesis is specifically about Tesla's Autopilot AI capability, its custom A4 chip, power efficiency, and the Optimus robotics extension — Tesla is unambiguously the direct subject.
